en: It's kind of hard to explain.
We learned these coordinates from an anomaly.
ko: 우리는 이상 현상에서 이 좌표를 배웠어요.

en: What sort of anomaly?
I hesitate to term it supernatural,
but it definitely wasn't scientific initially.
You're going to have to be specific, Mr. Cooper, right now.
ko: 구체적으로 말씀해 주셔야 합니다, 쿠퍼 씨, 지금 당장요.

en: It was gravity.
Um, what sort of gravitational anomaly?
What was this?
I'm real happy that you're excited about gravity, bud,
ko: 중력에 흥미를 가져서 정말 기뻐요, 친구,

en: but you're not getting any answers from us until I get assurances.
ko: 하지만 제가 보장을 받을 때까지는 답변을 드릴 수 없어요.

en: Assurances? Yeah.
Like that we're getting out of here,
ko: 우리가 여기서 나갈 수 있다는 것처럼요,

en: and I don't mean in the trunk of some car.
ko: 차 트렁크에 들어가는 게 아니라는 뜻이에요.

en: Don't you know who we are, Coop?
No, Professor, I don't.
You're not my father, Professor Brand.
ko: 당신은 제 아버지가 아니에요, 브랜드 교수님.

en: We're NASA.
NASA? NASA.
The same NASA you flew for.
NASA Jet Propulsion Laboratory, California Institute of Technology
ko: NASA 제트 추진 연구소, 캘리포니아 공과대학교.
